comparative_claim_requires: identical scene, camera, resolution, and sample conditions

claims.reason: The before/after checkpoint lacks a paired objective quality gate under identical conditions. The raw timings remain historical records only.

claims.recorded_result: schema-v3 warmup, percentile, provenance, memory, CSV, summary, and manifest records
claims.reason: The checkpoint explicitly makes no uplift claim and exists to validate measurement completeness.

claims.recorded_result: 29.1951/29.7210 ms versus prior 31.0712 ms protocol record
claims.reason: Repeated identical performance runs support a regression guard, while the temporal quality fixture uses a different scene and resolution and cannot support an uplift claim.

claims.recorded_result: 2.71/2.60 s GPU interop versus 9.28/9.32 s host readback
claims.reason: Both presentation paths consume the same rendered device frame at identical scene, cameras, resolution, sampling, and display policy; device-view and pixel-contract tests preserve output semantics. The metric includes process initialization.

claims.recorded_result: 0.527584 ms versus 0.592768 ms at 16 candidates, but MSE 0.025011288 versus 0.021958989
claims.reason: The candidate is about 11.0% faster in the current isolated run but has 13.9% worse MSE. The previous 1.15 tolerance contradicted VAL-03 equal-or-better quality.

claims.recorded_result: 18.4988 ms four-camera realtime; previously described as 34.0% lower than a 28.022 ms record
claims.reason: The current fixed-workload matrix is retained, but the 34.0% uplift wording is retired until the same smoke workload has an attributable before/after image-quality parity artifact.
This is a scoped preparation microbenchmark over the same 1,024 instances and prototype identity, not an end-to-end rendering uplift claim.

claims.recorded_result: two frames x eight candidates plus one previous-frame spatial donor; MSE ratio 0.783138517; four-batch mean 0.309995203 ms versus 0.563055995 ms baseline
claims.reason: Four independent five-trial RTX 3090 batches used the same 64-light scene, camera, 128x128 resolution, and 16 generated candidates per pixel. All 20 paired trials were faster and every batch passed strict quality, performance, temporal, spatial, reset, and reference gates.

nrd_relax_reblur.confirmed_finding: The provided integration targets D3D11, D3D12, or Vulkan through NRI or an application RHI and expects normal+roughness, view-Z, motion, separated diffuse/specular radiance, and hit-distance resources.
nrd_relax_reblur.product_gap: The renderer is CUDA/OptiX with OpenGL presentation and does not expose the required roughness, separated diffuse/specular, or hit-distance texture contract. Adding an RHI and guide contract would dominate a denoiser comparison and would not be a bounded experiment.

selected_experiment.confirmed_finding: RTXDI identifies temporal plus spatial resampling as central to noise reduction and recommends one to three spatial samples, while requiring explicit bias correction and correct previous-frame surface data.
selected_experiment.fit: The current CUDA path already has DI reservoirs, previous-frame positions, normals, material and primitive identity, so a previous-frame spatial-neighbor pass is bounded and RTX 3090 native.
acceptance: same 64-light scene, camera, 128x128 resolution, and 16-candidate budget as the conventional baseline
candidate render time below the conventional baseline in at least five repeated isolated runs
first-frame, disocclusion, scene-reset, and identity mismatch reuse remain invalid
default and public acceptance suites remain green
selected_experiment.failure_policy: Reject spatial reuse and close Phase 6 without advanced reuse if the strict quality/performance gate cannot be met without biased visibility shortcuts.
